title: Storage Spaces Direct (S2D) Storage Pool Utilization and Tier-Imbalance Detection
exclusions: Storage Spaces (non-Direct) single-server volumes; Storage Pools on JBOD enclosures attached to non-S2D Windows hosts; Hyper-V VHDX expansion at the file-system layer (separate use case); Azure-managed storage services (Azure Files, Azure NetApp Files, Azure Blob); Linux mdadm/btrfs/ZFS; SCM (Optane Persistent Memory) tiers used in App-Direct mode rather than as cache.
dataSources: `index=storage_s2d` (aliases `azure_stack_hci`, `hci_cluster`, `windows`) ingesting ConvertTo-Json snapshots from Azure Stack HCI 23H2 and Windows Server 2025/2022/2019 Datacenter Storage Spaces Direct clusters. `sourcetype=s2d:pool` captures Get-StoragePool FriendlyName, Size, AllocatedSize, OperationalStatus, HealthStatus, EnclosureAwareDefault, IsClustered, MediaTypeDefault. `sourcetype=s2d:virtualdisk` captures Get-VirtualDisk Name, FootprintOnPool, Size, AllocatedSize, ResiliencySettingName, NumberOfDataCopies, PhysicalDiskRedundancy, NumberOfColumns, OperationalStatus, HealthStatus. `sourcetype=s2d:tier` captures Get-StorageTier MediaType, Size, AllocatedSize, FootprintOnPool, ResiliencySettingName. `sourcetype=s2d:storagejob` captures Get-StorageJob Optimize/Rebuild/Repair/Regeneration/Reallocation PercentComplete fields. Optional `sourcetype=s2d:performancehistory` holds Get-ClusterPerformanceHistory slices; `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Health/Operational` carries Storage Health Service capacity faults. Stamp cluster_key consistently on every HEC payload so searches merge nodes cleanly.
app: `Splunk_TA_windows` ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) on each node for WinEventLog and perfmon parsing; add a small custom app such as TA_s2d_pool_capacity that schedules PowerShell collectors every 300–900 seconds into s2d:pool, s2d:virtualdisk, s2d:tier, and s2d:storagejob sourcetypes.
spl:
(index=storage_s2d OR index=azure_stack_hci OR index=hci_cluster OR index=windows) (sourcetype="s2d:pool" OR sourcetype="s2d:tier" OR sourcetype="s2d:virtualdisk" OR sourcetype="s2d:storagejob") earliest=-6h
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(Cluster_Name,cluster_name,fq_cluster,ComputerName,host)))
| eval pool_label=coalesce(PoolFriendlyName,FriendlyName,pool_name,PoolName,"")
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval pool_bytes=case(st="s2d:pool",tonumber(coalesce(Size,PoolSizeBytes,pool_size_bytes,"")),st="s2d:tier",tonumber(coalesce(Size,TierSizeBytes,tier_size_bytes,"")),null())
| eval alloc=tonumber(coalesce(AllocatedSize,allocated_bytes,TierAllocatedBytes,""))
| eval footprint_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(FootprintOnPool,footprint_on_pool,""))
| eval pool_usage_pct=if(st="s2d:pool" AND pool_bytes>0 AND isnotnull(alloc),round(100*alloc/pool_bytes,2),null())
| eval tier_fill_pct=if(st="s2d:tier" AND pool_bytes>0 AND isnotnull(alloc),round(100*alloc/pool_bytes,2),null())
| eval tier_media=coalesce(MediaType,media_type,"")
| eval resiliency=coalesce(ResiliencySettingName,resiliency_setting,"")
| eval vd_logical=tonumber(coalesce(Size,VolumeLogicalBytes,logical_size_bytes,""))
| eval jobnam=lower(coalesce(Name,JobName,job_name,""))
| eval optimize_pct=if(st="s2d:storagejob" AND match(jobnam,"(?i)optimize"),tonumber(coalesce(PercentComplete,percent_complete,"")),null())
| eval optimize_eta_sec=if(st="s2d:storagejob" AND match(jobnam,"(?i)optimize"),tonumber(coalesce(EtaSecondsRemaining,eta_seconds,"")),null())
| eval pool_warn=st="s2d:pool" AND pool_usage_pct>=70 AND pool_usage_pct<80
| eval pool_crit=st="s2d:pool" AND pool_usage_pct>=80
| eval map_footprint=st="s2d:virtualdisk" AND match(lower(resiliency),"(?i)mirroracceleratedparity") AND isnotnull(footprint_bytes) AND isnotnull(vd_logical) AND footprint_bytes>(vd_logical*1.25)
| eval parity_heavy=st="s2d:virtualdisk" AND match(lower(resiliency),"(?i)parity") AND isnotnull(NumberOfColumns) AND tonumber(NumberOfColumns)>=9
| eval optimize_slow=st="s2d:storagejob" AND isnotnull(optimize_pct) AND optimize_pct<95 AND optimize_eta_sec>14400
| eval tier_hot=st="s2d:tier" AND tier_media IN ("SSD","HDD","SCM") AND tier_fill_pct>=85
| where pool_warn OR pool_crit OR map_footprint OR parity_heavy OR optimize_slow OR tier_hot
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,max(pool_usage_pct) as peak_pool_usage_pct,max(tier_fill_pct) as peak_tier_fill_pct,values(resiliency) as resiliency_modes,max(optimize_pct) as optimize_percent_complete,min(optimize_eta_sec) as optimize_eta_seconds,dc(pool_label) as pool_scope,values(tier_media) as tier_breakdown,max(case(pool_crit OR pool_usage_pct>=80,1,0)) as breach_critical,max(case(optimize_slow,1,0)) as optimize_long_tail,max(case(map_footprint,1,0)) as map_volume_flag,max(case(parity_heavy,1,0)) as parity_layout_flag by cluster_key
| eval posture=case(breach_critical=1 OR peak_pool_usage_pct>=80,"capacity_critical",optimize_long_tail=1 OR map_volume_flag=1 OR parity_layout_flag=1 OR peak_tier_fill_pct>=85,"tier_ops_review",peak_pool_usage_pct>=70,"capacity_warning",1=1,"informational")
| sort -last_seen
| table cluster_key,posture,peak_pool_usage_pct,peak_tier_fill_pct,resiliency_modes,tier_breakdown,optimize_percent_complete,optimize_eta_seconds,pool_scope,last_seen
description: Tracks Microsoft Storage Spaces Direct storage-pool utilization using Get-StoragePool AllocatedSize versus Size, compares Get-VirtualDisk FootprintOnPool against logical Size across ResiliencySettingName patterns (Mirror, Parity, MirrorAcceleratedParity), surfaces Get-StorageTier slab pressure via AllocatedSize and FootprintOnPool per MediaType, monitors Optimize-StoragePool execution through Get-StorageJob telemetry, and optionally aligns WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Health/Operational faults so HCI teams rebalance slabs or expand shelves before OperationalStatus degrades.
value: Once pools exceed roughly 70–80% allocation, provisioning slows and MirrorAcceleratedParity landing footprints can spike unexpectedly; slab imbalance may concentrate load on fewer nodes even while individual disks remain healthy. Aligning FootprintOnPool with logical Size, column counts, mirror copies, and Optimize job timing gives storage owners runway to add capacity, reshape volumes, or finish slab moves before automated faults block new clustered shared volumes.
implementation: Install Splunk_TA_windows Health channel inputs, deploy Universal Forwarder scripts on a 5–15 minute cadence that emit Get-StoragePool, Get-VirtualDisk, Get-StorageTier, and Get-StorageJob JSON into the s2d:* sourcetypes, normalize cluster_key, schedule the packaged search with 70%/80% pool thresholds and long-running Optimize detection, and document when to run Optimize-StoragePool versus adding capacity shelves.
visualization: (1) Single-value peak_pool_usage_pct with 70/80 bands by cluster_key. (2) Stacked bars of tier_breakdown (HDD/SSD/SCM). (3) Table of resiliency_modes, optimize_percent_complete, optimize_eta_seconds, peak_tier_fill_pct. (4) Timechart overlaying s2d:pool pool_usage_pct series with Health faults. (5) Supplemental heatmap of per-node Get-PhysicalDisk AllocatedSize rollups sourced from extended collectors.

references.title: Plan volumes for Storage Spaces Direct (Microsoft Learn)
Fault tolerance and storage efficiency (Microsoft Learn)
Performance history for Storage Spaces Direct (Microsoft Learn)

knownFalsePositives:
Bulk VM template imports during tenant onboarding temporarily push AllocatedSize toward Size because golden images hydrate replicas quickly; suppress capacity_critical until a bulk_seed_campaign enrichment clears while Optimize and Rebuild queues remain healthy.

Optimize-StoragePool jobs deliberately reshape slabs: Get-StorageJob PercentComplete under 95 with multi-hour EtaSecondsRemaining can look like imbalance even though Get-StorageTier AllocatedSize ratios stabilize once Optimize completes.

Resiliency migrations (three-way Mirror toward MirrorAcceleratedParity) inflate FootprintOnPool transiently versus logical Size while NumberOfColumns changes finalize—delay tier_ops_review until Get-VirtualDisk OperationalStatus returns OK on every node.

Capacity-disk replacements invoke Rebuild, Regeneration, and Reallocation tasks that temporarily consume slack similar to exhaustion; confirm Repair-VirtualDisk timelines and verify there is no Lost Communication storm before reacting only to percentages.

Shrink-volume maintenance reduces logical Size while FootprintOnPool lags garbage collection, so mismatches resemble footprint faults until Optimize or Repair drains complete.

Thin-provision churn without tier edits can surface parity_heavy noise when NumberOfColumns spikes only because stats merged two FriendlyName collisions from throwaway volumes—deduplicate pool_label extraction and require two consecutive polling cycles before escalating.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Universal Forwarder on each **Azure Stack HCI 23H2** node and Windows Server 2025/2022/2019 Datacenter member participating in **Storage Spaces Direct**; endpoints must reach **HEC** receivers and service accounts must execute Storage cmdlets inside approved maintenance paths.
• Splunk_TA_windows ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) stays loaded so **WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Health/Operational** retains structured Health faults before custom JSON parsing lands.
• Route scripted telemetry into index `storage_s2d` with sourcetypes `s2d:pool`, `s2d:virtualdisk`, `s2d:tier`, `s2d:storagejob`; optional `s2d:performancehistory` mirrors **Get-ClusterPerformanceHistory** digests without doubling licensing charges.
• Grant **gMSA** rights per node and tee **PowerShell** stderr through a wrapper so silent exits surface inside **splunkd.log** before capacity telemetry disappears.
• Archive each stamp's layout choices (Mirror, Parity, **MirrorAcceleratedParity**, NumberOfColumns) because **FootprintOnPool** expectations shift with efficiency targets.

Step 1 — Scripted collection across pool, volume, tier, and job planes
(1) Collect-S2dPoolSnapshot.ps1 executes **Get-StoragePool** filtering **IsPrimordial** false, selects FriendlyName, **Size**, **AllocatedSize**, OperationalStatus, HealthStatus, **EnclosureAwareDefault**, **IsClustered**, **MediaTypeDefault**, emits JSON every 600–900 seconds to sourcetype s2d:pool, and stamps cluster_key via **Get-Cluster**.
(2) Collect-S2dVirtualDisk.ps1 serialises **Get-VirtualDisk** with **FootprintOnPool**, **ResiliencySettingName**, **NumberOfDataCopies**, NumberOfColumns, DetachedReason for sourcetype s2d:virtualdisk; keep CSV **Name** strings unique to avoid merged stats collisions.
(3) Collect-S2dTier.ps1 exports **Get-StorageTier** **MediaType**, **Size**, **AllocatedSize**, **FootprintOnPool** into s2d:tier so cache-tier versus capacity-tier **slab** pressure is visible—this differs from sibling **UC-19.3.7** NVMe reliability scraping.
(4) Collect-S2dStorageJob.ps1 captures **Get-StorageJob** rows whose **Name** references Optimize, Rebuild, Repair, Regeneration, or Reallocation including **PercentComplete** and **EtaSecondsRemaining** inside s2d:storagejob.
(5) Optional nightly **Get-ClusterPerformanceHistory** pulls (**-StorageSubsystem**, **-TimeFrame LastDay**) feed s2d:performancehistory as corroborating **IOPS** context—not the primary gate.

Step 2 — Saved search instantiation
Validate JSON keys using **| head 5** on each sourcetype, promote the SPL packaged in the **spl** field into a saved search **hci_s2d_pool_capacity_posture**, align cron with collector cadence, and macro-wrap index unions when tenants rename indexes.

```spl
(index=storage_s2d OR index=azure_stack_hci OR index=hci_cluster OR index=windows) (sourcetype="s2d:pool" OR sourcetype="s2d:tier" OR sourcetype="s2d:virtualdisk" OR sourcetype="s2d:storagejob") earliest=-6h
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(Cluster_Name,cluster_name,fq_cluster,ComputerName,host)))
| eval pool_label=coalesce(PoolFriendlyName,FriendlyName,pool_name,PoolName,"")
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval pool_bytes=case(st="s2d:pool",tonumber(coalesce(Size,PoolSizeBytes,pool_size_bytes,"")),st="s2d:tier",tonumber(coalesce(Size,TierSizeBytes,tier_size_bytes,"")),null())
| eval alloc=tonumber(coalesce(AllocatedSize,allocated_bytes,TierAllocatedBytes,""))
| eval footprint_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(FootprintOnPool,footprint_on_pool,""))
| eval pool_usage_pct=if(st="s2d:pool" AND pool_bytes>0 AND isnotnull(alloc),round(100*alloc/pool_bytes,2),null())
| eval tier_fill_pct=if(st="s2d:tier" AND pool_bytes>0 AND isnotnull(alloc),round(100*alloc/pool_bytes,2),null())
| eval tier_media=coalesce(MediaType,media_type,"")
| eval resiliency=coalesce(ResiliencySettingName,resiliency_setting,"")
| eval vd_logical=tonumber(coalesce(Size,VolumeLogicalBytes,logical_size_bytes,""))
| eval jobnam=lower(coalesce(Name,JobName,job_name,""))
| eval optimize_pct=if(st="s2d:storagejob" AND match(jobnam,"(?i)optimize"),tonumber(coalesce(PercentComplete,percent_complete,"")),null())
| eval optimize_eta_sec=if(st="s2d:storagejob" AND match(jobnam,"(?i)optimize"),tonumber(coalesce(EtaSecondsRemaining,eta_seconds,"")),null())
| eval pool_warn=st="s2d:pool" AND pool_usage_pct>=70 AND pool_usage_pct<80
| eval pool_crit=st="s2d:pool" AND pool_usage_pct>=80
| eval map_footprint=st="s2d:virtualdisk" AND match(lower(resiliency),"(?i)mirroracceleratedparity") AND isnotnull(footprint_bytes) AND isnotnull(vd_logical) AND footprint_bytes>(vd_logical*1.25)
| eval parity_heavy=st="s2d:virtualdisk" AND match(lower(resiliency),"(?i)parity") AND isnotnull(NumberOfColumns) AND tonumber(NumberOfColumns)>=9
| eval optimize_slow=st="s2d:storagejob" AND isnotnull(optimize_pct) AND optimize_pct<95 AND optimize_eta_sec>14400
| eval tier_hot=st="s2d:tier" AND tier_media IN ("SSD","HDD","SCM") AND tier_fill_pct>=85
| where pool_warn OR pool_crit OR map_footprint OR parity_heavy OR optimize_slow OR tier_hot
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,max(pool_usage_pct) as peak_pool_usage_pct,max(tier_fill_pct) as peak_tier_fill_pct,values(resiliency) as resiliency_modes,max(optimize_pct) as optimize_percent_complete,min(optimize_eta_sec) as optimize_eta_seconds,dc(pool_label) as pool_scope,values(tier_media) as tier_breakdown,max(case(pool_crit OR pool_usage_pct>=80,1,0)) as breach_critical,max(case(optimize_slow,1,0)) as optimize_long_tail,max(case(map_footprint,1,0)) as map_volume_flag,max(case(parity_heavy,1,0)) as parity_layout_flag by cluster_key
| eval posture=case(breach_critical=1 OR peak_pool_usage_pct>=80,"capacity_critical",optimize_long_tail=1 OR map_volume_flag=1 OR parity_layout_flag=1 OR peak_tier_fill_pct>=85,"tier_ops_review",peak_pool_usage_pct>=70,"capacity_warning",1=1,"informational")
| sort -last_seen
| table cluster_key,posture,peak_pool_usage_pct,peak_tier_fill_pct,resiliency_modes,tier_breakdown,optimize_percent_complete,optimize_eta_seconds,pool_scope,last_seen
```

**Field rationale**
– **pool_usage_pct** isolates rows where **st** equals s2d:pool so downstream **max()** reflects real pool percentages rather than tier-local ratios.
– **map_footprint** surfaces **MirrorAcceleratedParity** layouts whose **FootprintOnPool** exceeds logical **Size** thresholds after mirror landing overhead—not NVMe wear counters.
– **optimize_slow** pairs low **PercentComplete** values with long **EtaSecondsRemaining** horizons, a common harbinger of **slab** skew needing **Optimize-StoragePool** follow-up.
– **peak_tier_fill_pct** warns when individual **Get-StorageTier** media buckets cross 85% of their tier **Size** even if the aggregate pool still looks green.

Alerting guardrails: throttle capacity_warning notifications to once per twelve hours per cluster_key unless **peak_pool_usage_pct** jumps five points inside a single polling epoch; never throttle capacity_critical.

Step 3 — Console validation
(1) Compare inbound s2d:pool events against **Get-StoragePool** **AllocatedSize** / **Size** on a canary node; adjust **coalesce** helpers if byte field names drift between OS builds.
(2) Run **Get-VirtualDisk** with **Format-Table** including **ResiliencySettingName** to ensure Splunk text matches localized PowerShell spellings.
(3) Use **Get-StorageJob** filtered to Optimize to cross-check **PercentComplete** timestamps against s2d:storagejob arrivals.
(4) Pull **Get-StorageSubSystem | Get-StorageHealthReport** for pool faults; if Health ignores overage policies, subscribe **Microsoft-Windows-Health/Operational** before trusting SPL alone.
(5) Optional **Get-ClusterPerformanceHistory** reviews throughput bursts complementing capacity findings.

Step 4 — Operational packaging
(1) Dashboard Studio posture tiles comparing capacity_critical versus capacity_warning versus tier_ops_review should prove freshness faster than twice the scripted interval.
(2) Tie cluster_key rows to CMDB finance metadata so approvers understand whether to invoke **Optimize-StoragePool** or procure shelves.
(3) Multiple distinct FriendlyName pools raise architecture questions whenever **dc(pool_label)** increments unintentionally.
(4) Chain base searches on dashboards to spare redundant stats executions during executive fly-throughs.
(5) Keep screenshots tying **Optimize-StoragePool** guidance from Microsoft Learn beside Splunk panels for auditors.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting misleading modes

• **Silent JSON collectors** — PowerShell terminates before **ConvertTo-Json** flushes; require try/catch blocks tagging stderr with **S2D_POOL_SNAPSHOT**.

• **cluster_key collisions** — mismatch between **ComputerName** and **Get-Cluster** naming; hydrate nightly lookup tables.

• **AllocatedSize volatility** — transient dips during Repair jobs mimic relief; insist on consecutive breaches vs one-sample spikes.

• **map_footprint noise** — **FootprintOnPool** swings while Optimize relocates **slabs**; always proof against optimize_percent_complete.

• **parity_heavy thresholds** — tune NumberOfColumns comparisons against documented fault-tolerance minimums.

• **Health fault storms** unrelated to pools — correlate fault identifiers with **Get-StorageHealthReport** prose.

• **ConvertTo-Json depth** — nested DetachedReason blobs may require deeper **-Depth** or supplemental **spath** macros.

• Azure Stack HCI servicing pauses — maintenance suppressions belong in KV-backed suppression markers.

• **CSV quota overlays** — tenant quotas may scream full while hardware pools remain balanced.

• **Hyper-V checkpoints** — merges temporarily inflate footprints until **slab** compaction completes.

• **Field aliasing regressions** — rerun **fieldsummary** quarterly after cumulative updates.

• **Dual ingestion** — duplicated HEC deliveries inflate percentages unless deduped by _time; verify identical GUID inputs.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full the shared storage pool is and whether the space is spread fairly across tiers and nodes before everyday work suddenly cannot create new disks. We also catch long rebalance jobs that look like imbalance but are simply the cluster moving slabs to a safer layout.
hardware: Same physical hardware as UC-19.3.7 (S2D HCI nodes — Dell PowerEdge MX/R6515/R6525/R7615/R7625, HPE Apollo 4200/Synergy 480, Lenovo ThinkAgile MX1021, DataON HCI-X, Cisco UCS C240 M5/M6 HCI, Supermicro 2029U Hyper) but this UC focuses on the logical pool/volume/tier capacity-management layer rather than physical-disk wear. NVMe cache-tier (Intel Optane 905P, Samsung PM1735, Micron 7400) plus capacity-tier SATA SSDs or HDDs.
